summary: This notebook provides a complete training and inference pipeline for player trajectory prediction in the NFL Big Data Bowl competition. It combines a massive suite of hand-crafted features—including geometric baselines, GNN-lite neighbor embeddings, route clustering, and temporal lags—with a custom PyTorch Transformer model (`SpatioTemporalTransformer`) that predicts future X/Y displacements. The pipeline handles play direction unification, sequence padding, and separate coordinate prediction before averaging results across multiple cross-validation folds. Readers should take away how geometric rules can serve as a strong deterministic baseline, how attention pooling effectively captures trajectory dynamics, and how weighted neighbor embeddings can approximate spatial interactions without full graph networks.
competition_flow: Loads NFL tracking data, unifies play direction, computes 150+ hand-crafted features (geometric endpoints, GNN neighbor embeddings, route clusters, lags), feeds sequences into a PyTorch Transformer model to predict future X/Y displacements, and averages predictions across 5-10 cross-validation folds for final output.
data_processing: Play direction unification (flipping X/Y coordinates and angles for 'right' direction)
Padding sequences to a fixed window size
features_engineering: Geometric endpoint baselines (momentum projection, ball landing, mirror coverage)
GNN-lite weighted neighbor embeddings (dx/dy/dvx/dvy for allies/opponents within radius)
ensembling: Averages predicted X and Y displacements across 5-10 cross-validation folds, then converts unified coordinates back to original play direction.
insights: Geometric rules form a strong deterministic baseline that the model only needs to learn corrections for.
Attention pooling over temporal sequences effectively captures player trajectory dynamics.
GNN-lite weighted neighbor embeddings successfully encode spatial interactions without full graph neural networks.
Route clustering and lag features provide crucial context for player intent and recent motion.
